extra.reason: config.py, auth.py, dispatcharr_client.py, routes.py, main.py, requirements.txt written -- config.py trimmed to VOD-scoped fields, routes.py trimmed to auth+settings endpoints only, main.py carries the two VOD background tasks verbatim.

All 11 business logic files copied verbatim. Verified: py_compile passes, and every cross-module import (config.py/routes.py/dispatcharr_client.py/auth.py symbols) matches what the new minimal shell actually exports.

package.json (trimmed, no hls.js/mpegts.js), vite/tailwind/postcss/tsconfig configs, index.html, main.tsx, index.css (theme vars, confidence-badge CSS dropped as unused), lib/api.ts (vodmanager-session key), lib/utils.ts, components/ui/{button,card,input}.tsx (verbatim), Login.tsx (VOD Manager branding), Settings.tsx (trimmed to Dispatcharr conn + credentials, EPG fields dropped), App.tsx (settings-gate -> login-gate -> render, minimal top bar with Settings/Logout, no theme picker) all written. Also added the /settings/disconnect/ endpoint to backend routes.py for parity with Settings.tsx's disconnect button.
VodManager.tsx copied verbatim -- confirmed it only depends on already-ported @/components/ui/{button,card} and @/lib/api, no changes needed.
Closed

Dockerfile (multi-stage, mirrors epgmatcharr's pattern) and docker-compose.yml (port 8282, own named volume vod_manager_data) written and verified: docker build succeeds end-to-end, container starts cleanly on port 8282, and data persists correctly across container restart.

Full end-to-end verification passed: (1) docker build succeeds, (2) container starts on 8282 with both background tasks (catalog refresher, enrichment scheduler) confirmed scheduled and running via logs, (3) first-run flow verified (Settings page shows when unconfigured, credentials setup, login all work through the real browser UI), (4) added a provider + created a movie + added a source through the full UI stack -- all API calls succeed, (5) streaming code path (xc_server.py) executes cleanly (clean 401 for invalid creds, no crashes) -- full playback verification requires a real Dispatcharr M3U account (by design, same security gate as production), which wasn't available in this test environment but the code is byte-identical to the already-verified EPGmatcharr version, (6) data persists correctly across container restart via the named volume. Also cleaned up leftover EPGmatcharr branding (logger names, Plex/Emby client identifiers, one user-visible settings string) to VOD Manager.

VOD Manager successfully extracted into this standalone project. Backend (config/auth/routes/main.py + all 11 VOD business logic files), frontend (new Vite app scaffold + VodManager.tsx as main page), and Docker/docker-compose (port 8282) all built and verified working end-to-end.
Implemented and verified working end-to-end (download/restore/reset for all 3 components, live-tested against real data).

Built and verified live: _transcode_vod_stream in xc_server.py re-encodes to H.264/AAC on the fly for movie-source/series-source preview paths, confirmed working against a real Plex-sourced .avi file. Whole-file transcode (not HLS), on-demand (starts when Play is clicked, not pre-transcoded), scoped to the in-app preview endpoints only, not the public XC catalog stream endpoints (matches the original scope questions in this issue). Real seek support (HLS segmenting) remains open as vod_manager-1qk -- a jump-to-timestamp partial fix (fresh stream at a chosen offset via ffmpeg -ss, not mid-stream scrubbing) shipped instead as a cheaper stopgap for the actual workflow need (skip past an intro to verify a title).

Fully resolved. The single-instance coordination mechanism (_has_capacity/_live_viewer_count) turned out to already exist in the code but was unconfigured for any real provider -- fixed by configuring it for all 6 real IPTV providers with their actual subscription limits (ProviderA 18, ProviderB 20, ProviderC 5, ProviderD 7, ProviderE 4, ProviderF 6). The multi-instance gap (only checking one hardcoded Dispatcharr account) was the same root cause as vod_manager-e2m -- both fixed together via the new dispatcharr_connections/provider_live_accounts model: _has_capacity now sums live viewer counts across every linked live account on every connected Dispatcharr instance before comparing to a provider's real total limit. Verified live: a real stream request against ProviderB correctly queried its linked live-TV account's viewer count before proceeding.

Fixed together with vod_manager-hgl (same root cause: VOD Manager assumed exactly one Dispatcharr instance). New dispatcharr_connections table generalizes the single implicit connection into a real list; vod_sync.sync_provider now pushes each provider's max_streams to every connection with a vod_relay_account_id configured, not just one. Each connection gets its own separate Dispatcharr profile object (provider_sync_profiles, per-connection since profile ids aren't shared across separate Dispatcharr databases). Also added connect_dispatcharr_instance to automate adding a new instance end-to-end (creates the XC client + Dispatcharr-side M3U account + saved connection in one call) instead of the manual multi-step process this previously required. Verified live: migration correctly carried over the existing connection, all 8 providers sync cleanly to it.

Base integration confirmed solid and extensively exercised throughout the 2026-07-19/20 session: real Plex movies/series playing (including the transcoded fallback for a Plex-sourced .avi file, verified live with jump-to-timestamp), Activity-panel session tracking with Plex Now-Playing heartbeat reporting, real import/sync working. No concrete remaining polish item was ever specified beyond vague placeholders ('richer library-selection UI, transcode-profile tuning') -- closing per the issue's own note to close once the base integration is considered done. Reopen with a specific, concrete ask if a real gap surfaces.

Already fully built (checkboxes on each row, 'Place selected (N)' button, plus a 'place all filtered' variant for the whole-result-set case, both for Movies and TV Shows) -- matches the exact ask (search, select some/all, bulk-place into a category). Verified live end-to-end tonight: created a throwaway test category, bulk-placed 3 real movies into it via the actual API (POST /api/vod/movies/bulk-place/), confirmed all 3 landed, cleaned up. No further work needed.

Done: (1) README.md now documents TLS/reverse-proxy/VPN deployment guidance under Security and deployment. (2) Lockout thresholds (max attempts, window, duration) are now configurable via Settings -> Security, backed by config.py's get/save_lockout_settings() and a new GET/POST /api/vod/lockout-settings/ route; xc_server.py reads them through a 30s-cached _lockout_settings() instead of hardcoded constants. Verified live: lowered threshold to 3 attempts, confirmed a real lockout log line and a 4th request rejected as locked out, then restored defaults. (3) Admin visibility for locked-out IPs left as-is (container logs only) -- issue itself flagged this low priority. (4) In-memory/per-process lockout state left as-is -- issue itself judged this an acceptable trade-off.

Already fully built and confirmed working in production: TMDB Lists integration (config.py TMDB key, tmdb_sync.py fetch_list_items/sync_category/sync_all with exact tmdb_id matching, vod_routes.py sync-source/sync-now endpoints, VodManager.tsx 'TMDB Lists' card with Add-list form and per-category manual sync). This was the issue's own top-ranked recommendation ('start with TMDB Lists specifically -- sidesteps the hardest part'). Verified live against the user's real 'Steven's Movie Picks' list (TMDB list REDACTED_LIST_ID) via browser click -> Sync now -> log confirms match against the real pool. Corrected a stale docstring in tmdb_sync.py claiming a scheduled task already calls sync_all() (it doesn't yet -- tracked separately as vod_manager-tq3). Trakt/Simkl/MyAnimeList integrations from the original title were research options, not requirements -- not pursued since TMDB Lists alone satisfies the use case; can reopen a fresh issue if a non-TMDB source is specifically wanted later.

Built: per-provider-type configurable catalog refresh interval (XC/Plex/Emby/Jellyfin each independently tunable, addressing the original 18+ min Plex-scan-on-shared-6h-interval motivation), configurable enrichment TTL, and a new opt-in TMDB Lists auto-sync scheduler (previously manual-only). Backend: config.py get/save_refresh_settings(), vod_db.py new providers.last_catalog_refresh_at column + cached _refresh_settings()/get_enrichment_ttl_seconds()/get_catalog_refresh_interval_seconds()/mark_provider_catalog_refreshed(), main.py's _vod_catalog_refresher rewritten to poll every 5min and refresh only providers actually due per their type's interval (was: one shared 6h sleep for everything), new _tmdb_sync_scheduler task (disabled by default). API: GET/POST /api/vod/refresh-settings/. Frontend: new 'Refresh Schedule' Settings card (hours-based inputs, seconds under the hood). Verified live: py_compile + tsc clean, Docker rebuild, migration confirmed (last_catalog_refresh_at column present, NULL on existing rows), first-boot correctly flagged all 8 active providers as due (matching old always-refresh-on-fresh-state behavior), confirmed a provider (ProviderA) got stamped with a real timestamp after its refresh completed, POST/GET round-tripped custom values, browser-verified the Settings UI renders and reflects saved values, then restored sane defaults (6h all types, 24h enrichment, TMDB sync off).

Built the recommended path from the issue's own design: multi-credential auth already existed (xc_clients table); added a per-credential category-visibility allowlist (xc_clients.category_allowlist, comma-separated category ids, NULL = unrestricted so every existing client is unaffected). Enforced at every layer, not just catalog hiding: get_vod_categories/get_vod_streams/get_series_categories/get_series filtered, get_vod_info/get_series_info return empty for disallowed items, and all 8 streaming routes (movie/series placed-content play, preview by movie/episode id, preview by specific source, both transcoded variants) independently verify category membership before proxying -- so a restricted client can't reach disallowed content even via a direct/copied stream URL, not just have it hidden from browse. New vod_db.py helpers: get_movie_category_ids/get_series_category_ids, update_xc_client gained category_allowlist/clear_category_allowlist params. API: PATCH /api/vod/clients/{id}/ extended. Frontend: new 'Category access' column in Connected Instances with an expandable movie/TV checkbox picker (Save/Clear-allow-all/Cancel), Save disabled on an empty selection to prevent the empty-string-means-unrestricted footgun. Verified live end-to-end: created a real test client restricted to one category, confirmed get_vod_categories/get_series_categories/get_vod_streams/get_series all correctly filtered (1208 streams, all category 14), confirmed get_vod_info returns {} for a disallowed movie, confirmed the actual /movie/ stream route returns 206 for an allowed movie and 404 for a disallowed one, confirmed the preview/movie/ route (movie_id-based) also 404s for disallowed content, confirmed the existing unrestricted Dispatcharr-instance clients are completely unaffected (still 15 categories, still 206 on the same disallowed-for-restricted stream), then deleted the test client. README.md documents the feature. Left as noted in the original issue: whether/how this gets exposed beyond the local network is a deployment decision (same TLS/tunnel guidance as the rest of the XC exposure), not a new mechanism -- no additional work needed there.

Built the real fix described in the issue: ffmpeg now outputs a proper HLS playlist + .ts segments for the in-app test player instead of a single forward-only fMP4 pipe, giving genuine seek support. Backend (xc_server.py): per-session temp dir (tempfile.mkdtemp), ffmpeg run with -f hls -hls_playlist_type event -hls_list_size 0 (keeps all segments so backward seek works across everything encoded so far) and -hls_base_url embedding the client's own credentials + a random 128-bit session id, new routes /preview/{movie,series}-source-hls/.../index.m3u8 and /hls-segment/{username}/{password}/{hls_id}/{segment}, both re-validating auth AND the per-instance category allowlist from vod_manager-4zj (same access control now covers this path too). Session lifecycle: registered in the existing Activity panel (title suffixed '(HLS)'), Kill button now tears down HLS sessions directly (kill_session detects _hls_id and calls _stop_hls_session, since there's no single long-lived request loop to flag like the other stream kinds have -- ffmpeg writes segments independently of whether anyone's fetching them). Idle sessions (45s with no playlist/segment request) are torn down automatically by a new standalone hls_sweep_loop background task registered in main.py's lifespan -- not just an opportunistic check inside the request handlers, which would silently leak a running ffmpeg process and its temp directory forever if no further HLS traffic ever happened again (caught this exact gap live during testing: an opportunistic-only sweep left a session running 90+ seconds past its timeout with zero other HLS traffic; fixed by adding the dedicated background loop). Frontend: added hls.js dependency, VodPlayer gained a third playback mode alongside direct/transcoded with its own useEffect-managed Hls.js instance (falls back to native <video> src for Safari), wired into all 6 existing PlayButton call sites (Needs Review, movie list, movie source rows, series episode rows) alongside the existing transcode fallback -- offered as a separate button since HLS's slower start is a real tradeoff against the existing fast-start forward-only transcode, not a strict replacement. Verified live end-to-end against a real movie: playlist generation (six-plus seconds for ffmpeg to produce a real segment, confirmed valid MPEG-TS via ), segment fetch, Activity panel visibility, Kill-button teardown (confirmed via docker exec /proc scan: zero lingering ffmpeg processes, temp dir removed, segment 404s after kill), and the standalone idle-timeout sweep (confirmed a session with zero further requests was torn down automatically ~45-60s later with no manual kill, after first misdiagnosing this as broken because my own verification curls to the segment URL were themselves resetting the idle timer -- corrected by checking via docker exec/logs only). tsc + py_compile clean throughout, Docker rebuild clean. README.md documents the feature and its real tradeoffs (slower start, backward-seek-only-so-far, real CPU/disk cost while active).

Folded into the broader AI-assist feature built 2026-07-20 (Anthropic API integration), per explicit user direction to fold this in alongside AI-assisted smart categories. Implemented as text-based reasoning (item name + TMDB candidates' overview/cast/season-episode counts), not the originally-scoped vision/poster comparison -- a deliberate scope narrowing, agreed with the user, to avoid the unresolved design questions the issue itself flagged (vision provider choice, per-item cost/rate-limits across a large backlog, whether poster_url is even captured pre-enrichment). Also deliberately narrower on autonomy: this always returns a suggestion (best_match_index + reasoning + confidence) for the reviewer to weigh, never auto-resolves -- resolve/ remains a separate, explicit human action. New route: GET /needs-review/{content_type}/{item_id}/ai-suggest/, backed by ai_assist.suggest_year_review_match(), surfaced as an 'Ask AI' button in the NeedsReviewRow UI. Verified live with a real Anthropic API key: correctly picked between the 2005 animated and 2024 live-action 'Avatar: The Last Airbender' with sound reasoning and an honest 'medium' confidence rating. If true vision-based auto-resolve is still wanted later, this closes as superseded-by-simpler-solution, not as fully replacing that original idea -- a fresh issue would be the right way to pick that back up.

Built and verified live with a real Anthropic API key. Light mode: 'Suggest a category with AI' correctly returned a valid 3-condition rule (genre contains Action, year gte 1990, year lte 1999) for '90s action movies'. Heavy mode: 'AI Evaluate' on a throwaway test category with a genre='Science Fiction' pre-filter (4,377 total candidates, capped to 40 considered) correctly judged 5 real movies as fitting 'movies about time travel' (Stargate Continuum, Mr. Nobody, 2010, The Time Capsule, Planet Outlaws) -- 4/5 clearly correct, 1/5 (2010) a defensible-but-debatable edge call, consistent with the feature's own framing as an assist a human still reviews, not an authority. Test category deleted after verification. README.md documents all three AI assists (this issue's two plus the year-review one folded into vod_manager-bbz).

Fixed with two changes in xc_server.py + vod_db.py: (1) new vod_db.get_episode_export_rows_for_series() replaces the N+1 per-episode loop in get_series_info with a single bulk query (one SQLite round-trip instead of up to thousands). (2) player_api.php's entire action-dispatch body was extracted into a plain sync function (_handle_player_api_action) and run via asyncio.to_thread instead of inline in the async route handler; _authenticate's client-matching loop and record_xc_client_seen write were similarly moved off the event loop via asyncio.to_thread. Together these mean one client's heavy request (or a burst of them, like a full Dispatcharr catalog sync) can no longer block every other concurrent request. Verified live: fired a concurrent burst of 15 get_series_info requests against real large series (up to 719 episodes, Survivor) while polling a trivial unrelated endpoint (/api/auth/verify/) every ~1s -- all probes returned in 100-200ms throughout the burst (previously: total server freeze, confirmed via a 15s+ timeout on the same trivial endpoint and CPU-pegged R-state process before the fix). Confirmed get_series_info's actual response is unchanged (Survivor: 50 seasons, 719 episodes, matching pre-fix behavior) -- this was a performance/concurrency fix, not a behavior change. Also confirmed catalog listing (15 categories) and stream playback (206 partial content) still work end-to-end after the refactor.

Fixed: added poster_url/cast_list/director to the four export-row queries in vod_db.py (get_movie_export_rows, get_movie_export_row_by_stream_id, get_series_export_rows, get_series_export_row_by_export_id) and wired them into xc_server.py's get_vod_streams (stream_icon), get_vod_info (cover_big/cast/director), get_series (cover), get_series_info (cover/cast/director) -- replacing hardcoded empty strings. Verified live against the real production pool: 56,368/56,506 movie streams now return a real TMDB poster URL, 5,620/5,620 exposed series return cover art, and a spot-checked movie's get_vod_info correctly returns real cast and director strings alongside its poster. Coverage matches enrichment state (unenriched items still return empty, as expected -- nothing to show yet, not a bug) and will grow automatically as background enrichment continues.

Confirmed vestigial: vod_sync.py's provider-limit push was fully generalized to all Dispatcharr Connections back in vod_manager-e2m, and grepped every remaining usage of get_vod_xc_account_id/save_vod_xc_account_id -- only the already-executed one-time migration (_migrate_primary_dispatcharr_connection) still references it, nothing user-facing does. Removed the xc_account_id field and its intro paragraph from the frontend, removed the now-unused /api/vod/settings/ GET/POST route, VodSettingsRequest model, and their imports from vod_routes.py. Renamed the SectionCard from 'VOD Settings' to 'API Keys' since that's now its accurate, sole purpose (TMDB + Anthropic keys). Left config.py's get/save_vod_xc_account_id functions in place since the one-time migration still calls them on fresh installs -- pure internal plumbing, not user-facing, no reason to touch. Verified live: tsc clean, browser-confirmed the card renders correctly with no trace of the stale field.

Wrapped the Providers table in an overflow-x-auto container with a min-w-[1100px] on the table itself, matching the established 'wide content scrolls in its own container, page body never scrolls horizontally' convention already used elsewhere. Verified live via browser screenshot: table now shows its own horizontal scrollbar and stays fully contained within the SectionCard boundary instead of blowing out the page layout.

Replaced the fixed max-w-5xl wrapper with a responsive max-w-5xl xl:max-w-6xl 2xl:max-w-7xl mx-auto -- widens progressively on larger screens (capped, not unbounded, since some sections are prose/forms that would look bad stretched to full width) and actually centers the content (previously pinned to the left edge inside its full-width parent, wasting all the slack space on the right specifically). Verified live via browser at a 2400px viewport: content now uses noticeably more width and is properly centered with equal margins, versus the old narrow left-pinned layout.

Restored theme parity with EPGmatcharr: index.css already had all 4 themes' CSS variables in place from the extraction (dark/mid/light/mono, keyed by [data-theme]) -- just needed the picker UI and persistence wired up, mirrored directly from EPGmatcharr's App.tsx/EPGMatcher.tsx pattern (THEMES/Theme export, initTheme() reading localStorage on load, setTheme() updating both the DOM attribute and localStorage, a button group in the header). Used 'vodmanager-theme' as the localStorage key (VOD-Manager-scoped, not shared with EPGmatcharr). Verified live via browser: all 4 themes render correctly and distinctly (screenshotted Light and Mono), and the selection persists correctly across a full page reload.

The inline-editable vod_relay_account_id field (fix option a) already existed in the manual connections table -- the actual gap was purely visual (option b): nothing distinguished 'not yet configured' from 'configured', so a user had no way to notice a connection was silently doing nothing. Added a clear inline warning (AlertCircle + 'not syncing' text, full explanation in the tooltip) that appears whenever vod_relay_account_id is null, directly next to the editable field. Verified live: created a throwaway test connection with no relay account, confirmed the warning renders with the correct explanatory tooltip, then deleted the test connection. Option (c), a dedicated 'Test connection' action, was intentionally not built -- the warning plus the already-existing editable field fully addresses the reported problem (silent no-op with zero feedback); a connectivity-test button would be a nice-to-have, not required to close this gap.

Fixed immediately (live, unblocking playback right away) by PATCHing max_streams from 1 to 50 on both existing Dispatcharr M3U accounts (dispatch-test id=27, dispatch2 id=23) directly via their own APIs. Fixed at the code level by replacing the hardcoded max_streams: 1 in vod_sync.py's connect_dispatcharr_instance with a named _DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_MAX_STREAMS=50 constant, with a comment explaining why this needs to be generous (the real enforcement happens per-provider via profiles, this is just a top-of-funnel cap that should never itself be the bottleneck) -- so any future 'Connect a new instance' automation run creates the account with a sane default instead of reintroducing this bug. README.md's 'Connecting Dispatcharr instances' section now mentions the 50-stream default and why. Rebuilt and restarted the container. 50 was chosen as generously above any realistic single-household concurrent-VOD-stream count while still being a real (non-infinite) safety cap; the real per-provider ceilings already known and enforced (ProviderB=20, ProviderA=18, etc.) remain the actual meaningful limits.

Removed the legacy Dispatcharr URL/token form and Disconnect button from Settings.tsx entirely -- it's now purely account-level (set up/change login credentials, nothing else). Changed app gating: first-run now requires setting login credentials (with a 'Skip for now' option, since running without a login was already a supported state), not connecting Dispatcharr -- since the app is fully usable (add providers, curate a pool, serve it via Connected Instances) without ever configuring a Dispatcharr connection at all. Backend: removed require_configured entirely from routes.py (was 503'ing every /api/vod/* and /api/backup/* route until the legacy Dispatcharr config existed), simplified GET /api/settings/ to just {has_credentials}, removed the now-dead POST /settings/, /settings/test/, /settings/disconnect/ endpoints. Dispatcharr Connections stays exactly where it already lived -- in the main page, alongside Connected Instances and API Keys -- confirmed live via browser: gear-icon modal now shows only login credentials with a Back link, main page's Dispatcharr Connections card unchanged and unaffected. Verified an existing logged-in session continued working unaffected by the change (no forced re-auth), and a real vod_routes call (GET /api/vod/providers/) succeeds without any Dispatcharr-configured precondition.

Built and verified live via browser walkthrough. tsc clean throughout (verified after every incremental edit, not just at the end). Confirmed: tab bar switches correctly between all 4 tabs with the right cards under each (14 SectionCards remain, down from 16 -- exactly matching Categories+NeedsYearReview being replaced by modals, nothing else lost). Movies tab: Manage Categories modal shows the TMDB-synced 'Steven's Movie Picks' category alongside manual/smart ones (the original motivating problem), clicking View closes the modal, applies the category filter, and correctly shows just that category's one movie. Needs Review modal shows all 78 real flagged series items, scrollable. Grid view toggle: real posters render in a responsive tile grid with a placeholder icon for missing posters; clicking a tile opens the detail modal with full parity to list mode (sources, categories, add-source form, enrich button all present and functional). Reload-persistence confirmed for both tab selection and (implicitly, same mechanism) view mode. Curation & Maintenance and Configuration tabs both confirmed to render their full card sets (Providers/Orphan Checker/Rich Metadata/TMDB Lists and API Keys/Security/Refresh Schedule/Connected Instances respectively). Docker rebuild + container restart clean, live background enrichment continued working normally throughout.

Missing Artwork queue (paginated TMDB search + AI-suggest + manual poster URL entry, browse-and-fix same as Needs Review) and multi-provider AI support (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini with per-provider keys + model override) both built, deployed, and verified live via Playwright: TMDB search returned real results, resolve applied and removed item from queue (9047->9046), provider switch updated active provider + model default correctly.

Missing Artwork queue (paginated TMDB search + AI-suggest + manual poster URL entry, browse-and-fix same as Needs Review) and multi-provider AI support (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini with per-provider keys + model override) both built, deployed, and verified live via Playwright: TMDB search returned real results, resolve applied and removed item from queue (9047->9046), provider switch updated active provider + model default correctly.

Built, deployed, and verified live via Playwright + direct DB checks: scan found 4,322 movie duplicate groups (colon/dash/quote-style variants of the same title+year, e.g. Chip 'n Dale: Rescue Rangers, Spider-Man: No Way Home, the AMFAD example from earlier). Merge verified correct (APEX -> Apex: old row deleted, sources/categories moved). Also paginated the results client-side (20/page) after discovering 4,322 groups rendered flat made the page unusably slow -- confirmed via Playwright click timeouts before the fix, clean after.
Added bottom Pager (mirroring the existing top one) plus a page-size selector (25/50/100/200, persisted to localStorage) for both Movies and TV Shows lists/grids.
